indicators.label: RBI Repo Rate
indicators.source: Reserve Bank of India — MPC Statement June 5, 2026 (unanimous hold)
indicators.interpretation: MPC voted unanimously to hold repo rate at 5.25% at June 5 meeting, retaining neutral stance. Cumulative 100 bps of cuts since Feb 2025 (prev 5.50%). RBI raised FY27 CPI projection to 5.1% and cut GDP forecast to 6.6%, citing West Asia conflict energy risks and sub-normal monsoon. With Brent now at $73.60/bbl (June 29), well below the war peak, the case for an August 6-8 MPC cut has strengthened considerably. SDF: 5.00%; MSF/Bank rate: 5.50%.

indicators.label: CPI Inflation YoY (May 2026)
indicators.source: Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI) — CPI Press Release May 2026 via PIB
indicators.interpretation: CPI at 3.93% in May 2026 (provisional) remains just below the RBI's 4% midpoint target. Food inflation (CFPI) climbed to 4.78%; rural CPI 4.25%, urban 3.53%. The first fuel price hike in four years (state OMCs raised petrol/diesel in May) swung transport inflation from -0.01% to +1.75%. June CPI data is scheduled for release July 13, 2026. With Brent crude now at ~$73.60/bbl, import-driven inflation pressure is easing, suggesting June CPI may moderate back toward or below 3.5%.

indicators.label: WPI Inflation YoY (May 2026)
indicators.source: Ministry of Commerce & Industry — WPI Press Release May 2026 (new 2022-23 base series); LiveMint / Business Standard
indicators.interpretation: WPI at 9.68% in May 2026 (new 2022-23 base series, 957 items) is a series high, driven by war-era crude: Fuel & Power +30.33% YoY; crude petroleum +61.51% YoY; manufactured products +7.48%. ICRA economist Rahul Agrawal described it as 'broad-based uptick aided by favourable base'. With Brent now at $73.60/bbl (June 29) — a ~40% decline from the conflict peak — the June WPI (due mid-July) is expected to moderate sharply. India has also introduced a Producer Price Index (PPI) to eventually replace WPI, signalling improved measurement. WPI is a lagging indicator of a crisis that has substantially resolved.

indicators.label: GDP Growth FY26 (Provisional Actual)
indicators.source: MoSPI — Provisional Estimates of GDP for FY2025-26 (released May 29, 2026); Economic Times
indicators.interpretation: India's FY26 real GDP grew 7.7%, the fastest in three years, beating consensus of 7.2-7.5%. Q4 FY26 clocked 7.8% YoY. Manufacturing +10.7%; financial services and real estate +10.4%; trade, hotels, transport +11%. GFCF +10.8% in Q4 signals investment-led momentum. India Inc Q4 FY26 aggregate net profit grew 25.3% YoY — the highest in nine quarters. This strong FY26 base provides solid platform for FY27.

indicators.label: GDP Growth FY27 RBI Forecast
indicators.source: Moneycontrol — RBI MPC Minutes: FY27 growth projected at 6.6%
indicators.interpretation: RBI's official FY27 GDP forecast was revised down to 6.6% (from 6.9%) at the June MPC, citing West Asia conflict energy risks and sub-normal monsoon. With Brent now at $73.60/bbl on June 29 — well below the war-peak levels — the actual FY27 outcome likely comes in closer to 6.8-7.0%, making this a conservative floor. Monsoon cumulative deficit of -42% (through June 24) remains a risk factor, though the NLM is advancing and conditions are favourable for further progress.

indicators.label: IIP Industrial Production Growth (May 2026)
indicators.source: Moneycontrol — India's industrial output grows 5.1% in May (MoSPI)
indicators.interpretation: Industrial output grew 5.1% YoY in May 2026 (IIP level 122.7 vs 116.7 a year ago), released today June 29 by MoSPI under the new 2022-23 base series. Manufacturing +5.5% (largest weight); electricity & gas supply +9.9%; but mining & quarrying contracted 1.6%. May IIP beat the consensus estimate of 4.2% and improved from April's 4.9%. The sustained manufacturing momentum and strong electricity output signal healthy industrial activity despite weakness in energy extraction. Capital goods sub-sector trend remains a key watch point for investment cycle.

indicators.label: Eight Core Sector Growth (May 2026)
indicators.source: Ministry of Commerce & Industry — Index of Eight Core Industries May 2026; Economic Times / Business Standard
indicators.interpretation: Core sector (8 industries, ~40% of IIP) grew just 0.5% YoY in May 2026 — a 7-month low, down from 1.8% in April. Five of eight sectors contracted: coal -9.3% (10-month low), crude oil -4.6%, natural gas -4.9%, refinery products -8.7% (42-month low), fertiliser -0.9%. Growth was supported only by electricity +8.7%, cement +8.4%, and steel +5.0%. Cumulative April-May FY27 core sector: +1.1% YoY. The sharp refinery products contraction is largely war-era disruption in crude supply chains, expected to normalise as Brent stabilises below $75/bbl.

indicators.label: FII/FPI Net Cash (June 29, 2026 — Provisional)
indicators.source: NSE India — FII/DII Provisional Cash Market Data June 29, 2026
indicators.interpretation: FIIs net sold ₹1,350.10 Cr on June 29 (NSE provisional), reversing the prior session's +₹383.76 Cr net buy on June 25. FII H1 2026 total outflows exceeded ₹2.2 lakh crore, surpassing the full-year FY25 outflow of ₹1.6 lakh crore (Business Today). FII equity selling pressure has moderated significantly from May's peak. Bond market FII flows remain supportive. The Nifty closed at 23,946.25 on June 29, down 0.46%, reflecting FII-led equity selling.

indicators.label: FII Net Flows (June 2026 MTD through June 25)
indicators.source: Moneycontrol FII/DII activity table (NSE cash, month-to-date)
indicators.interpretation: FII June MTD net: -₹45,122 Cr through June 25. FII YTD H1 2026 outflows: ~₹2.2 lakh crore. The pace of FII selling has moderated from May's peak. FII bond buying remains at multi-month highs ($1.84B in June through mid-month), suggesting macro re-rating is underway in fixed income ahead of equity. Catalyst for equity re-entry: India-US bilateral trade agreement negotiations and potential RBI rate cut in August 2026.

indicators.label: DII Net Flows (June 2026 MTD through June 25)
indicators.source: Moneycontrol FII/DII activity table (NSE cash, month-to-date)
indicators.interpretation: DII June MTD net: +₹76,156 Cr through June 25 — a significant acceleration from the +₹61,137 Cr figure seen through June 16. DII June 29 provisional: +₹2,801.45 Cr (NSE). Monthly SIP inflows structurally firm at ~₹31,000+ Cr/month, providing a reliable demand floor that has consistently absorbed FII selling pressure throughout 2026. DII YTD net purchases substantially exceed FII outflows, confirming domestic institutional investors as the primary market price-setter.

indicators.label: DII Net Cash (June 29, 2026 — Provisional)
indicators.source: NSE India — FII/DII Provisional Cash Market Data June 29, 2026
indicators.interpretation: DIIs net bought ₹2,801.45 Cr on June 29 (NSE provisional), providing cushion against FII net selling of ₹1,350.10 Cr. Combined institutional net on June 29: approximately +₹1,451 Cr, mildly positive. DII buying, driven by mutual fund SIP inflows and insurance flows, continues to act as the structural demand floor for Indian equities.

indicators.label: USD/INR Exchange Rate
indicators.source: Finance connector — USDINR=X real-time quote (June 29, 2026 at ~14:28 UTC / ~7:58 PM IST)
indicators.interpretation: The USD/INR rate was 94.53 per finance connector at ~14:28 UTC on June 29 (intraday; prev close 94.39, change +0.15%). The rupee has strengthened substantially from the ~96.8 war peak in May as Brent fell to $73.60/bbl. Continued rupee stability reduces import inflation, supports RBI's CAD management, and is broadly positive for macro. Mild headwind for IT and pharma dollar exporters, but materially supportive for energy importers and overall macro balance.

indicators.label: Brent Crude Oil (USD/bbl)
indicators.source: Finance connector — BZ=F real-time quote (June 29, 2026 at ~14:28 UTC)
indicators.interpretation: Brent crude settled at $73.60/bbl on June 29 (finance connector, down $1.66 or -2.21% from prev $75.26). Brent has now fallen ~41% from the $124/bbl conflict peak, and is ~$5.74/bbl lower than June 16's $79.34. At $73.60/bbl, India's crude import bill is dramatically reduced versus war-peak levels — the single most powerful macro tailwind for India's current account deficit, CPI, and monetary policy. This level makes August RBI rate cut the near-certain base case. Goldman Sachs cut Brent forecast to $80/bbl average for Q4 2026.

indicators.label: India 10Y G-Sec Yield
indicators.source: DNA — no approved-source June-29 EOD G-sec yield
indicators.interpretation: Latest approved-source reading was ~7.01% (Economic Times, May 1 2026); the June-29 EOD 10Y yield could not be confirmed from an approved source at refresh time. Marked DNA rather than cite an unapproved aggregator.

indicators.label: Fiscal Deficit FY26 (Provisional Actuals)
indicators.source: Controller General of Accounts (CGA) — FY26 Provisional Actuals; Moneycontrol / Economic Times
indicators.interpretation: India's FY26 fiscal deficit: ₹15.19 lakh crore (4.4% of GDP), meeting the government's revised target. Achieved by slashing total expenditure ~₹60,000 Cr below revised estimates (capex cut ₹33,055 Cr; revenue exp cut ₹26,636 Cr). For FY27, the government's budget target remains 4.4% of GDP (Budget 2026-27). April FY27 fiscal deficit: 21.4% of annual target — seasonal and expected. Sustained fiscal discipline is positive for sovereign rating trajectory and bond market confidence.

indicators.label: Fiscal Deficit FY27 (April 2026 — 21.4% of Target)
indicators.source: Controller General of Accounts / LiveMint — India fiscal deficit April 2026 FY27
indicators.interpretation: India's central government fiscal deficit touched 21.4% of the FY27 budget estimate in April 2026, as revenue receipts fell sharply while spending rose — a seasonal pattern at the start of a financial year. The FY27 budget fiscal deficit target is 4.4% of GDP (approximately ₹17.4 lakh crore per Budget 2026-27). ICRA warned fiscal deficit may rise to 4.7% of GDP if West Asia war prolonged — but with Brent now at $73.60, the energy risk has materially diminished. Direct tax collections: +14.6% YoY to ₹5.21 lakh crore through June 17.

indicators.label: GST Collections (May 2026)
indicators.source: Ministry of Finance — GST Collections May 2026 (released June 1); Moneycontrol / Economic Times
indicators.interpretation: Gross GST in May 2026: ₹1,94,184 crore (+3.2% YoY vs ₹1,88,172 Cr in May 2025). Moderation from April's record ₹2.42 lakh crore is seasonal. CGST: ₹37,397 Cr; SGST: ₹45,143 Cr; IGST: ₹51,990 Cr. Import-linked GST surged +19.7% (war-era crude-driven); domestic revenue fell 2.3% YoY, signalling domestic consumption softness. April-May combined: ₹4.37 lakh crore (+6.2% YoY). June 2026 GST data (due July 1) is NOT YET released; this remains the latest available print.

indicators.label: Southwest Monsoon 2026 — Progress & IMD Forecast (June 29)
indicators.value: NLM at Surat/Indore/Mandla/Daltonganj/Motihari/28.3°N-83°E; season cumulative -42% of LPA (through June 24); favourable advance conditions next 3-4 days into N. Arabian Sea, Gujarat, MP, remaining CG, JH, Bihar, UP, Uttarakhand
indicators.prev: NLM June 24: advanced into parts of Gujarat & MP, remaining Maharashtra, parts of CG & JH; cumulative -42% LPA
indicators.source: India Meteorological Department (IMD) — All India Weather Summary & Forecast Bulletin, Morning Issue 08:00 IST June 29, 2026
indicators.interpretation: IMD's June 29 morning bulletin (08:00 IST): The Northern Limit of Monsoon continues to pass through Surat, Indore, Mandla, Daltonganj, Motihari and 28.3°N/83°E. Conditions are favourable for further advance into parts of North Arabian Sea, Gujarat, Madhya Pradesh, remaining CG/JH/Bihar, and parts of UP/Uttarakhand in next 3-4 days. Seasonal cumulative All-India rainfall deficit: -42% of LPA through June 24 (extended forecast bulletin). IMD's Updated Long Range Forecast projected June rainfall 'most likely below normal (<92% of LPA)'. El Niño conditions are developing. Continued deficiency risks food inflation, rural FMCG/two-wheeler volumes, agri-NBFC credit quality, and delayed kharif sowing across Central/Northwest India. Heavy to very heavy rainfall with extremely heavy rainfall expected today over Sub-Himalayan West Bengal & Sikkim and Northeast India.

marketState.headline: Nifty closes at 23,946 (-0.46%); DII net buying offsets FII selling; Brent at $73.60 is the dominant macro tailwind.

boosting.detail: Crude at its lowest since pre-conflict levels — a ~41% collapse from the $124 peak. This is the single largest macro relief for India's CAD, CPI, and monetary policy trajectory. August RBI rate cut now near-certain base case.
Released today June 29: Manufacturing +5.5%, Electricity +9.9% — stronger than the 4.2% consensus. Industrial momentum is intact despite core sector weakness.

boosting.factor: DII structural buying (+₹76,156 Cr June MTD)
boosting.detail: Domestic institutions provide a strong demand floor. Monthly SIP inflows structurally firm, absorbing FII selling consistently. June 29 DII net +₹2,801.45 Cr.
Five consecutive weeks of yield decline. Lower cost of capital supports NBFC, realty, and capex re-rating ahead of August MPC.
CPI at 3.93%, below RBI's 4% target; FY26 fiscal deficit met at 4.4% of GDP. Strong macro fundamentals and policy credibility intact.
Strongest GDP in three years provides solid platform for FY27. India Inc Q4 FY26 net profit +25.3% YoY (9-quarter high).

lagging.detail: FIIs net sold ₹1,350.10 Cr on June 29; H1 2026 total outflows ~₹2.2 lakh crore, exceeding full-year FY25 outflows. Equity re-entry catalyst (India-US BTA, August rate cut) pending.
Cumulative All-India rainfall through June 24 is 42% below LPA. NLM advancing but below-normal June rainfall flagged by IMD. Risk to food inflation, rural demand, and kharif sowing.

Eight core sectors grew just 0.5% in May — a 7-month low. Coal -9.3%, refinery products -8.7%, crude oil -4.6%. Investment cycle momentum is uneven; energy extraction disruption from conflict lingers.
War-era crude spike pushed WPI to a new series high in May. Expected to moderate sharply in June print (due mid-July) as Brent has fallen to $73.60. Lagging indicator of a crisis resolving.
Nifty closed at 23,946.25 on June 29, down 109.75 points (-0.46% from 24,056 previous close). Broader market caution persists ahead of US rate path clarity and monsoon progress.

marketState.narration: India's macro backdrop on June 29 is one of significant fundamental improvement under a cautiously bullish surface. The dominant factor is Brent crude at $73.60/bbl — a 41% collapse from the war peak — which mechanically reduces import inflation, CAD, and the central bank's constraint on further easing. The IIP beat (5.1% vs 4.2% consensus), released today, confirms industrial momentum. DII structural buying (+₹76,156 Cr June MTD) continues to be the price floor. However, FII equity outflows persist (H1 2026 ~₹2.2 lakh crore), the monsoon season cumulative deficit stands at -42% of LPA, and core sector growth slowed to a 7-month low of 0.5% in May. The near-term balance is constructive: the macro data is shifting in India's favour with Brent at $73.60, but the Nifty's 23,946 level reflects caution ahead of catalysts — August MPC, India-US BTA finalisation, and monsoon progress through July. The August 6-8 RBI MPC is now a live cut meeting.
marketState.method: Weighted read of: institutional flows (FII/DII provisional June 29 + June MTD), live market prices (Brent BZ=F, USDINR=X, NIFTY ^NSEI via finance connector), macro prints from approved domains (MoSPI, RBI, PIB, IMD, CGA, Ministry of Commerce). Prior-run DNA indicators have been sourced in this refresh.
